work_item.description: Non-development gate semantics: review agents may only veto COMPLETENESS, never the decision itself. For human-decision phases (discovery, event modeling, architecture, design system), the gate flow becomes: reviewer agent produces a findings list (inconsistencies, conflicts with accepted ADRs, missing sections) → kernel surfaces findings to the human via an ask_human step → the human decides accept / revise / reject. The kernel records the human's verdict, attributed to the human. Agents retain full gate authority only in development/review phases. Context: 2026-06-12 dogfooding — a codex adr_review gate autonomously vetoed the eventcore+SQLite ADR twice; the product owner's expectation is that reviewers raise findings for his decision, not make the decision.
